verdict_reasoning: EREG is a single-pass type I transmembrane protein with an extracellular EGF-like domain. It traffics to the cell surface via its own TM anchor, undergoes documented regulated ADAM-mediated ectodomain shedding at the cell surface, and maintains a stable cell surface pool (basolateral in polarized cells, or apical when its sorting motif is disrupted). The TM precursor stage is stable and functionally relevant, not merely transient. Characteristic of canonical EGFR family ligands.
